privacyPage.title: Privacy policy
privacyPage.noticeTitle: Before you rely on this policy
privacyPage.noticeBody: Complete all bracketed fields with accurate details. Apple App Privacy labels and Google Play Data safety should match this Policy and your live build (including Firebase push, map tiles, and optional website analytics). Update this Policy when you change SDKs or retention practices. Not legal advice.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.body:
1.1 Who we are

This Privacy Policy describes how [LEGAL_ENTITY_NAME], a [ENTITY_TYPE] registered in the Republic of North Macedonia with registration number [REGISTRATION_NUMBER] and registered office at [REGISTERED_ADDRESS] ("Chisto.mk," "we," "us," or "our"), collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal data when you use our mobile applications (iOS and Android) and our public website (collectively, the "Platform").

We operate Chisto.mk as a civic environmental initiative focused on transparency and community reporting about local environmental conditions.

Most account, reporting, and map interaction features run in the apps. The website is mainly marketing, legal information, and contact; it may use a small number of cookies or local storage as described in our Cookie Policy.

Effective date: [EFFECTIVE_DATE].

1.2 Our commitment

We are committed to protecting your privacy and complying with applicable data protection laws, including:

Law on Personal Data Protection of the Republic of North Macedonia (Official Gazette No. 42/2020)
Regulation (EU) 2016/679 - General Data Protection Regulation ("GDPR")
Law on Electronic Communications (Official Gazette No. 39/2014)

1.3 Contact information

Data controller:
[LEGAL_ENTITY_NAME]
[REGISTERED_ADDRESS]
Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Phone: [CONTACT_PHONE]
Data protection officer (if applicable):
Email: [DPO_EMAIL]
Supervisory authority:
Agency for Personal Data Protection
Republic of North Macedonia
Website: https://www.dzlp.mk
Email: info@dzlp.mk

sections.title: 2. Scope and application
sections.body:
2.1 This Privacy Policy applies to personal data we process when you:

Use the Chisto.mk mobile apps
Use the website at [WEBSITE_URL] (including forms or newsletters we operate)
Email or otherwise contact us

It also covers processing by subprocessors we use to host or operate the service (listed in general terms below and in agreements we maintain).

2.2 This Policy does not govern:

Independent websites or apps you open via links from us (their policies apply)
Processing by public authorities or third parties that act on their own legal basis
Content you exchange with other users outside our systems

2.3 By using the Platform, you confirm that you have read this Policy. Some processing relies on consent (for example optional marketing or optional analytics on the website); you may withdraw consent where the law allows, as described below.

sections.title: 3. Personal data we collect
sections.body:
We collect personal data to run the civic map, authenticate users, moderate content, and improve reliability. Categories include:

3.1 Data you provide directly

3.1.1 Account data (apps)

First and last name
Email address
Mobile phone number (used for sign-in and verification with one-time codes)
Password (we store a secure hash, not the plain password)
Optional profile photo and in-app preferences
Optional multi-factor authentication data if you enable it
Gamification fields tied to your account (for example points), where the product includes them

3.1.2 Reports and sites

When you submit a pollution report we collect the report text, category and similar fields you enter, photos you attach, coordinates and address or place text for the location, timestamps, and moderation outcomes. Reports are linked to a "site" record on the map. Other users may see public report and site content according to product rules.

3.1.3 Community and events

Comments and likes on sites, saves, and share actions you trigger
Cleanup events shown in the product (scheduled time, linked site, organiser fields the UI collects, participant counts)

3.1.4 Support and contact

What you send us by email, contact forms, or comparable channels

3.1.5 Payments

We do not operate in-app purchases or paid subscriptions in the current product. If we introduce paid features later, we will update this Policy and name the payment processor.

3.2 Data collected automatically

3.2.1 Technical and security data

IP address, device type, operating system and app or browser version where available
Session or device records we need for security (for example refresh token identifiers, optional device description)
Server and application logs for reliability and abuse detection

3.2.2 Usage data

Interactions needed to operate features you use (for example opening a report, voting, commenting)
Optional product analytics if implemented in the apps (we will describe the vendor here when enabled)

3.2.3 Location

Precise location when you choose to attach a location to a report or use map features that require it. We do not intend to track your location continuously in the background; if that ever changes, we will ask for a separate permission and update this Policy.

3.2.4 Push notifications

If you opt in, we store a push device token (for example via Firebase Cloud Messaging) and related preference flags so we can deliver notifications you request.

3.2.5 Website cookies and storage

The public website uses strictly necessary storage plus, if you agree, Vercel Web Analytics. Details are in the Cookie Policy.

3.3 Third-party sources

We may use mapping or geocoding services (for example Carto basemaps or Esri imagery in the apps, and geocoding helpers) to render maps or resolve addresses; those providers may receive technical requests needed to load tiles or geocode coordinates. We do not operate social login in the current apps; if we add it, we will update this section.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis for processing
sections.body:
Under GDPR Article 6, we rely on:

4.1 Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))

Operating accounts, publishing reports you submit, showing map content, comments, events, and notifications you ask for.

4.2 Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f))

Securing the service, preventing abuse, debugging and reliability, internal statistics that do not require marketing profiles, and limited product improvement, balanced against your rights.

4.3 Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))

Accounting, tax, or regulatory duties where they apply, and lawful requests from authorities.

4.4 Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))

Where required, for example optional marketing communications, optional website analytics cookies, or any future feature we offer only on an opt-in basis. You may withdraw consent without affecting earlier processing that was lawful.

4.5 Vital interests (Art. 6(1)(d))

Rare processing needed to protect life or serious safety.

sections.title: 5. How we use your personal data
sections.body:
5.1 Running the Platform

Authenticate you, maintain your profile, and operate map, report, comment, event, and notification features you use.

5.2 Safety, trust, and moderation

Review reports of abuse, enforce our Terms, protect users, and keep infrastructure stable.

5.3 Communications

Send service messages (for example security notices or report status) using contact details you provide. Marketing or newsletter messages only where we have a lawful basis (usually consent).

5.4 Aggregates for partners

Produce statistics that do not identify individuals where we describe in agreements or product copy.

5.5 Legal and compliance

Meet retention duties, respond to lawful requests, and defend legal claims.

5.6 Improvement

Understand errors and performance in aggregate; we avoid unnecessary profiling beyond what the product needs.

sections.title: 6. Data sharing and disclosure
sections.body:
6.1 Consent or instruction

We share data with third parties when you ask us to or when you deliberately make content public in the product.

6.2 Infrastructure and operations

We use vetted processors, for example:

Hosting and API infrastructure: [HOSTING_PROVIDER] in [SERVER_LOCATION] (or as updated in our records)
Email delivery for website contact forms and update notifications: [EMAIL_PROVIDER]
SMS verification: Twilio where SMS one-time codes are active
Push notifications: Google Firebase Cloud Messaging for device delivery
Mobile diagnostics and analytics: Firebase Crashlytics and Firebase Analytics when included in the production app
Website analytics (if you opt in on the site): Vercel Web Analytics
Support channel: [SUPPORT_PROVIDER]

Contracts require processors to use data only on our instructions and to apply appropriate security.

6.3 Maps and geolocation helpers

Map tiles may be loaded from Carto (OpenStreetMap-based raster tiles) and, where the UI offers it, Esri World Imagery or similar imagery providers. Geocoding may use platform APIs you already bundle. Those providers receive technical requests (tile coordinates, IP in server logs) as normal for map delivery.

6.4 Authorities and aggregates

We may share anonymised or aggregated environmental statistics with municipalities or partners. We may disclose identifiers or content if the law compels us, to protect rights and safety, or to enforce our Terms.

6.5 Reorganisation or succession

If we merge, restructure, or another organisation continues the service, personal data may transfer to the successor. We will require them to respect this Policy or notify you.

6.6 Public visibility

Reports, comments, usernames, and event listings you publish may be visible to other users and, depending on configuration, on the open web.

sections.title: 7. International data transfers
sections.body:
7.1 Data storage locations

Your personal data is primarily stored on servers located in [SERVER_COUNTRY/REGION].

7.2 Transfers outside EU/EEA

If we transfer data to countries outside the European Economic Area that do not have an adequacy decision from the European Commission, we ensure appropriate safeguards:

EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
Binding Corporate Rules (where applicable)
Recipient's certification under approved frameworks
Your explicit consent for specific transfers

7.3 Macedonian context

As a candidate country for EU membership, the Republic of North Macedonia aligns its data protection framework with EU standards. We treat data protection with the same rigor as EU member states.

sections.title: 8. Data retention
sections.body:
We keep personal data only as long as needed for the purposes above or as the law requires. Exact schedules evolve with the product; the following reflects our current intent.

8.1 Accounts and security

Profile and credentials: while your account is active, then typically up to 90 days after closure unless law or dispute resolution needs more time.
Sessions and security logs: long enough to detect abuse (often months, not years, unless an investigation needs longer).

8.2 Reports, sites, and community content

Environmental reports may stay visible as part of the civic map for a long time or indefinitely because they document conditions in the public interest. If you exercise erasure rights, we may remove or anonymise your personal link to a report (for example detaching your account) while keeping non-personal location and imagery that cannot identify you, unless law requires otherwise. We handle erasure and anonymisation requests in line with this description.

Photos and rich media: retained with the report until deletion or anonymisation rules apply.
Comments, votes, saves: retained until you delete them, we moderate them away, or the related site is removed according to product rules.

8.3 Events

Cleanup events stay linked to sites for scheduling history; any participant lists follow the same principles as account data.

8.4 Marketing and contact history

Marketing consents and proof of opt-in or opt-out: as required by law, usually a few years after the last message.

8.5 Legal, tax, and backups

Records we must keep for accounting, tax, or litigation hold periods set by Macedonian law.
Backups rotate; purging from backups may lag production deletion by up to 90 days.

8.6 Inactive accounts

We may warn you before deleting long-inactive accounts, then remove personal data subject to the report anonymisation rules above.

sections.title: 9. Your rights under GDPR
sections.body:
Under GDPR and Macedonian data protection law, you have the following rights:

9.1 Right of access (Article 15)

You have the right to obtain:

Confirmation whether we process your personal data
A copy of your personal data
Information about processing purposes, categories, recipients, and retention periods

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with subject "Data Access Request"
Response time: Within 30 days (may extend to 60 days for complex requests)
Cost: Free for the first request; reasonable fee for excessive requests

9.2 Right to rectification (Article 16)

You have the right to correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data.

How to exercise:

Update directly through account settings in the apps where available
Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with correct information
Response time: Without undue delay, maximum 30 days

9.3 Right to erasure / "Right to be forgotten" (Article 17)

You have the right to request deletion of your personal data when:

Data is no longer necessary for original purposes
You withdraw consent (where consent was the legal basis)
You object to processing and there are no overriding legitimate grounds
Data was unlawfully processed
Legal obligation requires deletion

Exceptions: We may refuse deletion when required for:

Legal compliance (e.g., tax records)
Legal claims defense
Public interest or scientific research

How to exercise: Account settings > Delete Account in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Response time: 30 days; data purged within 90 days including backups

9.4 Right to restriction of processing (Article 18)

You have the right to limit processing when:

You contest data accuracy (during verification)
Processing is unlawful but you oppose deletion
We no longer need data, but you need it for legal claims
You objected to processing (pending verification of legitimate grounds)

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with specific restriction request
Effect: Data marked and stored, but not actively processed

9.5 Right to data portability (Article 20)

You have the right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller.

Applies to:

Data provided by you
Processing based on consent or contract
Processing carried out by automated means

How to exercise: Account settings > Export Data in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Format: JSON, CSV, or PDF
Response time: 30 days

9.6 Right to object (Article 21)

9.6.1 Marketing

You have the absolute right to object to direct marketing.

How to exercise: Click "Unsubscribe" in emails, or update preferences in account settings in the apps where available

9.6.2 Legitimate interest processing

You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interests.

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with specific objection
Effect: We will cease processing unless we demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds

9.6.3 Profiling and automated decision-making (Article 22)

We do not currently engage in automated decision-making with legal or significant effects. If this changes, you will have the right to:

Obtain human intervention
Express your point of view
Contest the decision

9.7 Right to withdraw consent

Where processing is based on consent, you can withdraw at any time without affecting prior lawful processing.

How to exercise: Account settings > Privacy Preferences in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]

9.8 Right to lodge a complaint

You have the right to file a complaint with the supervisory authority:

Agency for Personal Data Protection
Republic of North Macedonia
Address: [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_ADDRESS]
Website: https://www.dzlp.mk
Email: info@dzlp.mk
Phone: [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_PHONE]

For EU residents: You may also lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your EU member state of residence.

sections.title: 10. Data security
sections.body:
We implement technical and organizational measures appropriate to the risk, taking into account the nature of the processing and the state of the art.

10.1 Measures we may use

Depending on how the service is hosted and developed, measures may include:

Encryption of personal data in transit (for example TLS)
Passwords stored using strong one-way hashing
Access controls for systems and accounts, including multi-factor authentication for administrative access where we deploy it
Patching, monitoring, and logging for reliability and abuse detection
Safeguards against common application risks (for example input validation and protections against injection and cross-site scripting where applicable)

We review and adjust controls as the service evolves.

10.2 Organization and vendors

We maintain practices suited to our size and risk, including how we work with hosting and other processors we engage.

10.3 Personal data breaches

If we become aware of a breach that is likely to affect your rights and freedoms, we will notify the supervisory authority without undue delay and within 72 hours where the GDPR or applicable law requires it, and we will inform affected users when the law requires that too.

10.4 Your part

Use a strong unique password, enable multi-factor authentication in the apps if offered, sign out on shared devices, and contact us promptly if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.

sections.title: 11. Children's privacy
sections.body:
11.1 The Platform is not intended for children under 18 years of age.

11.2 We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18 without parental consent.

11.3 If we become aware that we have collected data from a child under 18 without verified parental consent, we will delete that information as quickly as possible.

11.4 If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided personal data to us, please contact us at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

11.5 Parents may supervise minors' use of the Platform, but minors may not create accounts independently.

sections.title: 12. Cookies and tracking technologies
sections.body:
The public website uses strictly necessary storage plus optional Vercel Web Analytics if you opt in through our banner. We do not run separate functional or marketing cookie toggles on the site today. Full detail is in the Cookie Policy at [COOKIE_POLICY_URL].

The mobile apps use on-device storage, push tokens, and map caches as described in Sections 3 and 6, not browser cookies.

sections.title: 13. Third-party links and services
sections.body:
13.1 The website may link to social profiles or partner pages. The apps load map tiles and geocoding from providers such as Carto (OpenStreetMap data) or Esri where offered.

13.2 Those services have their own privacy notices. We do not control them.

13.3 We do not embed social login in the current mobile product. If we add integrations, we will list them here.

sections.title: 14. Changes to this Privacy Policy
sections.body:
14.1 Notification of changes

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you by:

Email notification to registered users (at least 30 days before changes take effect)
Prominent notice on the Platform
In-app notification
Updated "Last Updated" date at the top of this Policy

14.2 Your acceptance

Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy. If you do not agree with changes, you must stop using the Platform and may close your account.

14.3 Version history

We maintain a version history of this Policy. Previous versions are available upon request at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

sections.title: 15. Specific provisions for Macedonian users
sections.body:
15.1 Language

This Privacy Policy is provided in Macedonian, English, and Albanian. In case of conflict, the Macedonian version prevails for users in the Republic of North Macedonia.

15.2 Supervisory authority

The competent supervisory authority for data protection matters in Macedonia is:
Агенција за заштита на личните податоци
(Agency for Personal Data Protection)
Address: [AUTHORITY_ADDRESS]
Website: https://www.dzlp.mk
Email: info@dzlp.mk

15.3 Legal basis

Processing of personal data is conducted in accordance with:

Закон за заштита на личните податоци (Official Gazette No. 42/2020)
EU GDPR principles as adopted in Macedonian legislation

sections.title: 16. Contact us
sections.body:
For questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or your personal data:

Privacy inquiries:
Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Phone: [CONTACT_PHONE]
Address: [REGISTERED_ADDRESS]
Data protection officer (if applicable):
Email: [DPO_EMAIL]
General contact:
Email: [CONTACT_EMAIL]
Website: [WEBSITE_URL]
Response time: We aim to respond to all privacy inquiries within 48 hours and resolve requests within 30 days.

BY USING THE PLATFORM, YOU CONFIRM THAT YOU HAVE READ THIS PRIVACY POLICY. WE PROCESS PERSONAL DATA AS DESCRIBED HEREIN, INCLUDING PROCESSING THAT DOES NOT RELY ON CONSENT WHERE THE LAW ALLOWS OTHER LEGAL BASES.

cookiesPage.title: Cookie policy
cookiesPage.noticeTitle: Before you rely on this cookie policy
cookiesPage.noticeBody: What the live website does (consent storage, optional analytics identifiers) should match this table and the Privacy Policy. Mobile apps use on-device storage and push tokens as described there, not this browser-focused summary. Bracketed URLs are set through your deployment environment. Not legal advice.
cookiesPage.tableIntro:
This table describes technologies on the public Chisto.mk website. The apps use on-device storage and Firebase messaging tokens instead; see the Privacy Policy.

BY CONTINUING TO USE OUR WEBSITE, YOU ACKNOWLEDGE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTOOD THIS COOKIE POLICY.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.body:
Effective date: [EFFECTIVE_DATE].

1.1 About this policy

This Cookie Policy explains how [LEGAL_ENTITY_NAME] ("Chisto.mk," "we," "us," or "our") uses cookies and similar storage on the Chisto.mk marketing website. Mobile apps follow the Privacy Policy for device storage, push tokens, and map caches.

Read this Policy together with:

• Privacy Policy: [PRIVACY_POLICY_URL]
• Terms of Use: [TERMS_URL]

1.2 Legal framework

We comply with the ePrivacy framework as transposed in Macedonia, the GDPR where it applies, the Law on Personal Data Protection (Official Gazette No. 42/2020), and the Law on Electronic Communications (Official Gazette No. 39/2014).

1.3 Consent on the website

Strictly necessary storage works without consent. Optional Vercel Web Analytics loads only if you accept analytics in our banner. You can reopen choices via Cookie settings in the footer ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]).

sections.title: 2. What are cookies?
sections.body:
2.1 Definition

Cookies are small files a site stores in your browser. Local storage is a related browser feature we use to remember your analytics choice.

2.2 Duration and party

• Session cookies expire when you close the browser.
• Persistent cookies or storage entries stay until they expire or you clear site data.
• First-party entries are set by our site. Third-party scripts (Vercel Analytics, when enabled) may set their own identifiers under Vercel's policies.

2.3 Mobile apps

Apps use secure storage, SQLite or file caches, Firebase Cloud Messaging tokens, and similar tools described in the Privacy Policy rather than browser cookies.

sections.title: 3. Why we use cookies
sections.body:
On the website we use storage to:

• Remember whether you accepted or rejected optional analytics
• Keep the site working (security, load balancing, and similar basics handled by our host)
• If you opt in, measure aggregate traffic with Vercel Web Analytics

We do not use marketing or social advertising pixels on this site today.

sections.title: 4. Categories we use on the website
sections.body:
4.1 Strictly necessary storage

We store your analytics choice in browser localStorage (key chisto_cookie_consent_v1) so the banner does not reset every visit and so we can respect rejections. Our hosting provider may also set short-lived technical cookies for security or delivery. Legal basis: legitimate interests and, where applicable, provision of the service you request.

4.2 Optional analytics (consent)

If you opt in, we load Vercel Web Analytics to collect aggregate page views and performance metrics. Legal basis: consent. You can withdraw consent anytime via Cookie settings ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]).

4.3 What we do not use here

We do not operate a separate marketing pixel, Facebook Pixel, or Google Ads tags on this marketing site in the current build. If that changes, we will update this Policy and the banner before enabling them.

sections.title: 5. Mobile applications
sections.body:
5.1 Technologies

The apps store login tokens securely, cache map tiles for performance, keep preferences locally, and register Firebase Cloud Messaging tokens when you enable notifications.

5.2 Advertising identifiers

We do not use the advertising ID to serve third-party ads in the current product. OS-level ad settings still apply generally on your device.

5.3 Permissions

We request access to location, camera, photos, and notifications only when a feature needs them. You can change permissions in device settings.

sections.title: 6. How to manage cookies
sections.body:
6.1 Banner and footer

On your first visit we ask whether to enable optional analytics. You can reopen the choice from "Cookie settings" in the footer ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]). The marketing site does not expose account-level cookie menus.

6.2 Browser controls

You may clear or block cookies and site data in your browser; blocking everything can break the site.

6.3 Do Not Track

Browsers may send DNT signals; we do not treat DNT as a binding legal request today because industry practice is inconsistent.

sections.title: 7. Cookie lifespan
sections.body: Consent records in localStorage remain until you clear site data or we bump the storage version. Vercel Analytics identifiers follow Vercel's documentation (often well under two years). Hosting cookies are typically short session cookies.

sections.title: 8. Social links
sections.body: The website may show ordinary links to social networks. Simply visiting our pages does not embed social login. If you click through to a network, that network's policy applies.

sections.title: 9. Data protection and security
sections.body:
9.1 Security

We serve the site over HTTPS and limit access to analytics dashboards.

9.2 Retention

Analytics data is handled under Vercel's terms. When you withdraw consent we stop loading their script on future page loads.

9.3 Transfers

Vercel may process data in the United States or other regions. We rely on appropriate safeguards described in their documentation and our Privacy Policy.

sections.title: 10. Your rights
sections.body:
Under the GDPR and applicable local law, you have rights regarding data collected through cookies, including:

• Right of access: Information about cookie-related data we hold
• Right to erasure: Deletion of cookie data where applicable
• Right to object: Object to processing based on legitimate interests where provided by law
• Right to withdraw consent: At any time for non-essential cookies
• Right to lodge a complaint: With a supervisory authority

How to exercise your rights:

• Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
• Cookie settings: [COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]

Supervisory authority (Macedonia):

Agency for Personal Data Protection. Website: https://www.dzlp.mk. Email: info@dzlp.mk

sections.title: 11. Children's privacy
sections.body:
11.1 Our Platform is not directed at children under 18 years of age.

11.2 We do not knowingly collect cookie data from children under 18.

11.3 If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has been tracked through cookies without appropriate consent, contact us at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

sections.title: 12. Updates to this policy
sections.body:
12.1 Policy changes

We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in our use of cookies, new legal requirements, improvements to the Platform, or user feedback.

12.2 Notification

We may notify you of material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top, email (for significant changes where appropriate), a prominent notice on the Platform, or an updated cookie consent banner.

12.3 Your acceptance

Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy. If you do not agree, adjust your cookie settings or stop using the Platform.

sections.title: 13. Contact information
sections.body:
Questions about cookies:

• Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
• Phone: [CONTACT_PHONE]
• Address: [REGISTERED_ADDRESS]

Data protection officer (if applicable):

• Email: [DPO_EMAIL]

General contact:

• Email: [CONTACT_EMAIL]
• Website: [WEBSITE_URL]

cookieRows.name: chisto_cookie_consent_v1 (localStorage)
cookieRows.purpose: Stores your analytics opt-in or opt-out so the banner can stay aligned with your choice.
cookieRows.duration: Until you clear site data or we change the storage key

cookieRows.name: Vercel Web Analytics identifiers
cookieRows.purpose: Aggregate page views and performance metrics when you opt in to analytics.

cookieRows.name: Hosting and security cookies
cookieRows.purpose: Delivery, routing, bot protection, or TLS session continuity as implemented by the host.

cookieRows.name: Next.js / application cookies (if any)
cookieRows.purpose: Short-lived first-party cookies may be used where needed for forms, sessions, or routing on the website.

dataPage.title: Your data and account
dataPage.lead: This is Chisto.mk's public account deletion and data rights page. It explains how to request deletion of your account and associated personal data, export your data, correct mistakes, object or restrict processing where applicable, and contact us about privacy questions.
dataPage.requestIntro: Use the channel below to request account deletion, report anonymisation where applicable, a copy or export of your personal data, corrections, restriction, objection where applicable, or other privacy support. Email requests go to info@ekohab.mk. We may ask for reasonable proof of identity, such as an email from your registered address or a one-time verification step, before acting on sensitive requests.
dataPage.requestMissing: The data request channel is temporarily unavailable. Email us at info@ekohab.mk.
dataPage.noticeTitle: Before you rely on this page
dataPage.noticeBody: This page is the public web channel for account deletion and privacy requests. Apple App Privacy and Google Play Data safety answers should match this page and the Privacy Policy. Not legal advice.

sections.title: 1. Account and profile
sections.body:
Accounts are created in the Chisto.mk mobile apps with your email, mobile phone number, password, and phone verification using one-time codes. Keep credentials secret.

Update profile fields inside the app where offered. Use a strong unique password. If the app offers authenticator-based MFA, turning it on reduces takeover risk.

The public website may not provide full registration; use the apps for account features.

sections.title: 2. Viewing and exporting your data
sections.body:
You may have the right to obtain confirmation of processing and a copy of your personal data (GDPR Art. 15). Where technically feasible, we provide self-service views in the product; otherwise submit a request via the channel above.

For data portability (GDPR Art. 20), you may request a structured, commonly used, machine-readable export (for example JSON or CSV) of data you provided and that we process by automated means based on contract or consent. We will provide it within a reasonable period, typically within thirty (30) days of verification unless applicable law requires a shorter or longer deadline.

sections.title: 3. Corrections
sections.body: If personal data is inaccurate or incomplete, you may request rectification (GDPR Art. 16) using the same channel. We will correct obvious errors promptly; complex disputes may require additional verification.

sections.title: 4. Account deletion and what we keep
sections.body:
You may request deletion of your Account (GDPR Art. 17) via the data request channel or in-app settings if available. We review verified deletion requests within 30 days. After approval, we remove or anonymise personal data in production systems except where retention is necessary: for example to comply with accounting or tax law, to establish or defend legal claims during applicable limitation periods, to prevent abuse, or to meet a specific legal obligation. Backups and security archives may take up to 90 days to expire or be overwritten. We will explain any limited retention if it applies to you.

Some environmental report content you published may remain visible without personal identifiers, because civic environmental observations can document conditions in the public interest. Where possible, we detach your account or other personal identifiers while keeping non-personal report facts.

sections.title: 5. Marketing and communications preferences
sections.body:
Service-related messages (security, reports, events) may be necessary.

Newsletters or marketing need a lawful basis, usually consent. Unsubscribe with the link in each email or contact us. Website contact and update notification emails are delivered through Resend today. Mailchimp will be used only if we later enable a newsletter list, and this page and the Privacy Policy will be updated before that happens.

sections.title: 6. Cookies and similar technologies
sections.body: On the website, use Cookie settings in the footer to allow or refuse optional Vercel Web Analytics. Strictly necessary storage still applies. See the Cookie Policy for detail.

sections.title: 7. Response times, appeals, and complaints
sections.body:
We aim to respond to verified requests without undue delay and within any statutory deadline (for example one month under the GDPR, with possible extensions for complex requests).

If you are unhappy with our response, you may complain to [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_NAME] or your local data protection authority. You may also seek judicial remedy where applicable law allows.

common.readTimeFormatted: {minutes, plural, one {# min read} other {# min read}}
common.feedbackPrompt: Did this article answer your question?
common.feedbackThanks: Thanks. Your feedback helps us improve these guides.
common.feedbackNoFollowupHint: Optional: one line about what felt unclear or incorrect. We do not collect personal data in this box.
common.feedbackReasonPlaceholder: Example: Step 3 does not match the screen I see after updating the app.
common.feedbackContactIntro: Prefer a reply from the team?

hub.title: Help centre
hub.subtitle: Practical guides for using Chisto.mk on the web and in the app: map and sites, reporting, cleanups, safety, offline use, your account, and fixes when something breaks.
hub.empty: No guides match that search. Try shorter words, or jump to a category below.
searchTips: Try everyday words: report, site, map, cleanup, account, password, notification.
If nothing matches, browse the topic cards by category.
Long guides have a table of contents at the top of the article.
hub.searchAnnounceResults: {count, plural, one {# guide matches your search.} other {# guides match your search.}}
hub.featuredIntro: New to Chisto.mk? Open one of these first, then explore the rest at your pace.
hub.catalogMetrics: {count, plural, one {# guide · same terms as in the app} other {# guides · same terms as in the app}}

getting-started.cardSummary: Install the app, create your account, pick a language, and learn where notifications and privacy settings live.
getting-started.title: Set up Chisto.mk

blocks.text: Chisto.mk runs on iOS and Android. Install from the App Store or Google Play, or tap the download buttons on the chisto.mk home page.

blocks.title: Before you install
items: Search the store for «Chisto.mk» and check the publisher matches our official listing.
On a limited data plan, use Wi-Fi. The first launch may download map data.
Keep the OS updated so the camera, maps, and security patches stay current.

blocks.text: If the store page is unavailable in your region, open chisto.mk on a desktop browser and use the official store links from there.
In these guides, words like report, site, and cleanup follow the same meanings as in the app so you always know which screen we mean.

Open the app and complete sign up. You need a phone number you can verify by SMS (or a similar one-time code), an email you read regularly, your name, and a password that meets the app rules. The same expectations are spelled out in our Terms of use.

blocks.title: What to prepare
items: A phone number you can access for the verification code.
An email inbox you check regularly for account notices.
A strong password that you do not reuse on other services.

blocks.text: Keep your device clock accurate. Large clock skew can sometimes interfere with code-based sign-in.
Where the app offers it, you can change the interface language. Map data focuses on Macedonia, but you still place each report exactly where it belongs.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Switch languages in settings if several people share one device.
If text looks untranslated, update the app. Translations ship with releases.
Drop the pin on the real location you mean, not a random city centre unless the whole area is affected.

blocks.text: You control whether the app may send push notifications in your device settings and in the app where offered. Our Privacy Policy explains how personal data is processed when you report issues or join events.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: iOS: Settings → Notifications → Chisto.mk.
Android: Settings → Apps → Chisto.mk → Notifications.
In-app toggles may exist for specific categories such as events or report status.

report-a-site.cardSummary: Capture useful photos, place the pin accurately, and follow the review journey after you send it.
report-a-site.title: File a pollution report

blocks.text: You get the best results on site with recent photos. Stay out of danger: traffic, unstable ground, and unknown liquids are never worth a closer shot.

blocks.title: Safety checklist
items: Stand away from traffic, unstable edges, and unknown liquids or sharp metal.
If wind or sun hurts readability, change angle rather than stepping closer to danger.
If you only have old photos, say so in the description so moderators understand context.

blocks.text: Never enter private property without permission. Capture from a public vantage when possible.
Add up to five photos. Start wide for context, then move closer to the problem: piles, pipes, stains, or debris. Pair images with a short headline and a factual description.

blocks.title: What moderators look for
items: Daylight and a steady hand make verification faster for moderators.
Include scale cues when size is unclear, for example a common object or a person at safe distance.
Mention smells, colours, or water flow only as observations, not medical claims.

blocks.text: Move the pin to the exact polluted spot inside Macedonia. Zoom until the building, shoreline, or roadside matches what you see on the ground.

blocks.title: Pin quality
items: GPS helps, but confirm visually because multipath errors happen near cliffs and tall buildings.
If the address field exists, keep it consistent with the pin so partners can route field teams.
When reporting a whole area, zoom out only as far as needed to describe the extent truthfully.

blocks.text: Every report is reviewed before it is shown publicly. Track status in My reports while moderators work through the queue.

blocks.title: What to expect
items: Public pins help neighbours and organisers coordinate, but they do not force a municipality to act on a fixed schedule.
Edits may be possible while moderation is open; check the row for the report in the app.
If something urgent threatens health or safety, also use official emergency channels where appropriate.

join-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Browse cleanups near you, RSVP, and follow organiser guidance before, during, and after the shift.
join-a-cleanup-event.title: Join a cleanup event

blocks.text: Open the Events area in the app to browse cleanups in Macedonia, read organiser notes, and join when registration is open.

blocks.title: Before you travel
items: Confirm the meeting pin, start time in local time, and how long the shift runs.
Check difficulty and terrain so clothing and footwear match the day.
See whether registration closes early or needs extra details from you.

blocks.text: Tap Join on an open event to reserve a spot. Organisers may post updates or reminders through notifications.

blocks.title: Stay on top of plans
items: Add the event to your calendar once you commit.
Turn on notifications if the organiser posts last minute changes.
Cancel your RSVP promptly if you cannot attend so headcounts stay accurate.

blocks.text: Follow the organiser safety plan, respect private property, and keep the group aligned with public access rules.

blocks.title: On site
items: Wear gloves or high vis gear only when the listing asks for them.
Supervise children closely and keep phones away from heavy lifting unless photos are welcome.
Ask before photographing people, vehicles, or homes.

blocks.text: After the event, add after photos if the flow invites them and leave short feedback so future volunteers know what to expect.

blocks.title: Wrap up well
items: Upload through the event flow when it is exposed in the app.
Avoid faces or licence plates unless organisers explicitly allow them.
Thank hosts through the in-app channel when one is available.

account-and-data.cardSummary: Protect your login, understand what we store, and use the right pages for privacy and data requests.
account-and-data.title: Your account, privacy, and data requests

blocks.text: Your Chisto.mk account connects the reports you file, events you join, and notification preferences so civic work in Macedonia stays coherent.

blocks.title: What may be linked to you
items: Sign in identifiers such as email or phone number.
Approximate map pins and descriptions from reports you submit.
RSVP status, attendance notes, and organiser messages for events you join.

blocks.text: Treat the account like a mailbox for civic issues. Strong access controls protect you and the map data others rely on.

blocks.title: Everyday hygiene
items: Use a unique password or your OS password manager.
Keep screen lock enabled on phones that leave the house with you.
Sign out before handing a device to someone else.

blocks.text: You can ask what we hold, correct mistakes, export a copy, or request deletion, subject to lawful exceptions such as moderation records.

blocks.title: How to start a request
items: Use in-app account settings wherever they are available.
Email support if a control is missing or the flow errors.
Expect identity checks when requests are sensitive or high risk.

blocks.text: Binding legal wording lives on dedicated pages. Read them when you need certainty beyond these guides.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: Privacy explains what we collect and why.
Terms explain acceptable use of the platform.
The data page summarises retention and rights paths in one place.

notifications-in-the-app.cardSummary: Choose which updates you get for reports, cleanup events, and account activity.
notifications-in-the-app.title: Notifications in the app

blocks.text: Chisto.mk can notify you when something important happens: for example a report you follow changes status, an event you joined is updated, or your account needs attention.

blocks.title: Typical channels
items: Push notifications on your phone when you allow them.
In-app banners or badges where the product surfaces them.
Email or SMS for account security or things you explicitly opt into.

blocks.text: Your phone always wins. If push is off at the OS level, Chisto.mk cannot override that. Use both OS settings and in-app toggles so alerts match how you work.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: iOS: Settings → Notifications → Chisto.mk.
Android: Settings → Apps → Chisto.mk → Notifications.
Inside Chisto.mk, open settings and review each category you still want, such as events or report updates.

blocks.text: Batching alerts keeps organisers from waking volunteers and keeps residents from feeling watched. Turn off what you do not need, especially overnight.

blocks.title: Practical habits
items: Use Focus modes or Do not disturb on iOS, and similar modes on Android, before sleep.
Mute long threads inside events if the organiser enables quieter updates.
Re-enable notifications before big cleanup days so last minute changes reach you.

blocks.text: Some messages are required for security, such as password resets or login codes. Marketing style messages only arrive when you clearly opt in.

blocks.title: Stay in control
items: Keep your email inbox reachable so you can recover access if you lose your phone.
If SMS codes stop arriving, check spam filters, carrier blocks, and whether roaming is on.
For how data is processed, read the Privacy policy linked below.

troubleshooting.cardSummary: Fix sign-in, map location, camera uploads, and know when to update or contact support.
troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting common issues

blocks.text: Most login issues come down to connectivity, clock skew, or an outdated build. Work through the list below before you assume the password is wrong.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Turn airplane mode off, wait a few seconds, then reconnect to Wi-Fi or mobile data.
Set date and time to automatic. Large drift can break SMS or email codes.
Request a fresh code, wait for it, and paste carefully. Codes expire quickly.
Update Chisto.mk from the store, then restart the app.

blocks.text: If the app crashes on launch, note your OS version and app version before you contact us. That detail speeds up support.
The map needs location access when you place or verify pins. If the blue dot drifts or disappears, fix permissions before you file a report.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: iOS: Settings → Privacy & Security → Location Services → Chisto.mk → While using the app.
Android: Settings → Location → App permissions → Chisto.mk → Allow only while in use when possible.
Open sky for a few seconds so GPS can settle, especially between tall buildings.
Disable mock location or developer GPS spoofing tools while using Chisto.mk.

blocks.text: If the pin still lands wrong after GPS settles, move it manually and describe why in the report text.
Large photos and weak networks do not mix. Give uploads time, or capture a smaller photo when the queue keeps failing.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Check that Chisto.mk has camera and photo library permission.
Free a little storage space on the device.
Switch to Wi-Fi for multi photo uploads.
Force close the app once, reopen, and retry the same report draft if it is still there.

blocks.text: Avoid starting a second report for the same issue while the first upload still shows pending.
When nothing above works, collect a short description and reach out. Reinstalling is a last resort because it can clear local drafts.

blocks.title: Before you write to us
items: Restart the phone once.
Confirm you are on the latest Chisto.mk build from the official store.
Capture screenshots of any error text (blur personal details first).

exploring-the-map.cardTitle: Use the map and site pages
exploring-the-map.cardSummary: Understand pins, open a site page, and read public information before you report or join a cleanup.
exploring-the-map.title: Use the map and site pages

blocks.text: The map is a shared civic view of reported places in Macedonia. Each pin is a site where someone documented an environmental concern.

blocks.title: Quick orientation
items: Zoom until street names and landmarks match what you see outside.
Tap a pin to open the site page with photos, descriptions, and history when available.
Newer activity may appear after moderation, so counts can change over time.

blocks.text: A site page summarises what the community submitted. It is meant for transparency, not for assigning blame to individuals.

blocks.title: What you can rely on
items: Photos and text come from contributors and moderators, not automated enforcement.
Timestamps help you understand recency when they are shown.
If something looks wrong, you can often add a new report rather than arguing in text.

blocks.text: Do not treat map pins as legal proof on their own. Use official channels for regulated decisions.
Use the map while stationary or as a passenger. Keep awareness of traffic and private property boundaries.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Prefer daylight for first visits to unfamiliar industrial edges.
If a path feels unsafe, step back and capture from a distance.
Respect no entry signs even when rubbish is visible beyond them.

blocks.text: Open a report when you can add fresh evidence or correct a pin. Duplicates without new facts slow moderation for everyone.

blocks.title: Before you start
items: Gather photos and a clear description of what changed since the last visit.
Move the pin to the exact spot that needs attention.
Mention safety limits you observed so organisers can plan.

trust-safety-and-moderation.cardSummary: Why moderation exists, how to contribute respectfully, and where the binding legal text lives.
trust-safety-and-moderation.title: Trust, safety, and moderation

blocks.text: Chisto.mk publishes civic environmental signals. Moderation reduces spam, protects people from harassment, and keeps the map readable for partners and volunteers in Macedonia.

blocks.title: What reviewers look for
items: Evidence that matches the location and time window you describe.
Respectful language without personal attacks.
Duplicates folded into the same site when that keeps the story clearer.

blocks.text: Assume good intent from neighbours and organisers. Focus on conditions you can photograph or describe factually.

blocks.title: Community norms
items: No doxxing: avoid posting private addresses for people instead of sites.
No hate speech or coordinated brigading.
Do not upload imagery that sexualises minors or glorifies self harm.

blocks.text: If tempers rise, pause and capture facts only. Moderators prioritise safety over speed.
Multiple reports about the same place can help show persistence, but identical copies add noise. Prefer updates with new angles or dates.

blocks.title: Practical guidance
items: If a pin is slightly wrong, file a correction through the reporting flow when the product allows it.
If categories are wrong, choose the closest honest label and explain in text.
Expect status labels to lag briefly while humans review.

blocks.text: This article summarises behaviour expectations. Binding rules sit in the legal pages linked below.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Privacy explains data categories and retention at a high level.
Terms explain acceptable use and enforcement levers.
The data page lists rights requests for account holders.

hosting-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Plan logistics, manage sign ups, keep people safe on the day, and close the loop afterwards.
hosting-a-cleanup-event.title: Host a cleanup event

blocks.text: Organisers set the tone. Clear logistics reduce no shows and keep volunteers safer around roads, water edges, and weather shifts in Macedonia.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: Pick a meeting pin, duration, difficulty, and any kit you will supply.
Confirm land access with owners or municipalities when required.
Publish contact expectations, for example whether phones stay on silent.

blocks.text: Use notifications thoughtfully. Short updates beat long threads when plans change.

blocks.title: Operational tips
items: Close registration early if capacity is limited.
Send a reminder the evening before with parking and toilet notes.
After cancellations, refresh headcounts so materials match reality.

blocks.text: Brief everyone on hazards, buddy pairs, and what to do if someone feels unwell.

blocks.title: On site leadership
items: Keep first aid basics and water visible.
Rotate heavy lifting roles and watch for heat stress.
Photograph results only where volunteers consent.

blocks.text: For emergencies, use official emergency channels alongside any in-app notes.
Close the loop with thank you notes, photo uploads if your flow supports them, and honest feedback for the next crew.

blocks.title: Wrap up
items: Archive chat decisions so future hosts learn from your runbook.
Log issues that need municipal follow up separately from volunteer chatter.
Celebrate wins briefly so newcomers see momentum.

offline-and-slow-networks.cardSummary: Understand pending uploads, retries, and when to wait instead of duplicating a report.
offline-and-slow-networks.title: Offline and slow networks

sections.title: How the app uses the network

blocks.text: Maps, photos, and submissions sync when connectivity returns. Some screens cache tiles so you can browse lightly offline.

blocks.title: Expectations
items: Account actions usually need a live session.
Draft fields may save locally until you submit.
Large photos take longer on 3G; queue them instead of forcing retries in a tunnel.

blocks.text: Pending means the device is still sending data or the server has not confirmed receipt. It is normal briefly after switching from Wi Fi to mobile data.

blocks.title: If you are stuck
items: Open the report summary and look for retry or resume prompts.
Avoid duplicating the same submission while pending is spinning.
Capture smaller photos if uploads fail repeatedly.

blocks.text: International roaming can block background uploads. Toggle data for the app explicitly if your OS allows per app cellular control.

blocks.title: Travel tips
items: Finish uploads before flights when possible.
Reconnect to Wi Fi before editing long descriptions.
If time zones shift, double check scheduled event reminders.

blocks.text: If the app crashes twice while uploading, pause and collect diagnostics such as OS version and app version before contacting support.

blocks.title: Escalation
items: Try restarting the device once.
Reinstall only after backing up credentials.
Use the contact page on this site for persistent blockers.

partnerships-for-organisations.cardSummary: How municipalities, NGOs, and journalists can collaborate with Chisto.mk without surprises.
partnerships-for-organisations.title: Partnerships for municipalities, NGOs, and media

blocks.text: This article is for organisations that want to use public map data, coordinate cleanups, or explore collaboration. It is not legal advice and does not replace your own counsel or procurement rules.

blocks.title: Typical readers
items: Municipal environment desks planning field follow up.
NGOs mapping volunteer capacity next to reported sites.
Journalists verifying locations before publishing.

blocks.text: Gather a short summary of your goal, the geography you care about, and any timeline. If you need bulk data or recurring exports, say how the public benefits and how you will protect personal data you might receive.

blocks.title: Helpful attachments
items: Official letterhead or email from an organisational domain when possible.
One paragraph on intended use of map or event information.
Preferred language for replies: Macedonian, Albanian, or English.

blocks.text: Binding rules live in our Terms, Privacy policy, and data pages. The help centre explains how the product behaves for residents; legal language stays on those policy pages.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Use the data page for high level descriptions of what the platform publishes.
Use the privacy page before you process any information that might identify individuals.
Link staff to the help hub so field teams share the same vocabulary as app users.

verifying-sites-in-the-field.cardSummary: Plan a safe visit, compare the pin to what you see, and file a new report when the evidence clearly changed.
verifying-sites-in-the-field.title: Verifying pollution sites on the ground

blocks.text: Open the site page in the app or website, note the pin, photos, and timestamps, then plan daylight access from a public vantage. Tell a colleague where you are going if you work alone.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: Charge your phone and download offline tiles if connectivity is weak.
Bring a notebook for observations you cannot photograph safely.
Avoid private land unless you already have permission.

blocks.label: Use the map and site pages

blocks.text: Stand where the map suggests and scan for the same cues shown in photos: kerb lines, trees, buildings, or shoreline bends. Small offsets happen; large mismatches deserve a careful new report rather than a comment alone.

blocks.title: When to file again
items: The hazard moved materially since the last photos.
The original pin sits on the wrong block or riverbank.
You can document a regulated risk that was not visible earlier.

blocks.text: Do not enter unstable piles, fast water, or industrial yards without training. Treat residents and workers courteously; the map is a civic space, not a place for confrontation.
If you discover an immediate danger to life, use official emergency channels first, then document for the map when it is safe.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
